Title: Submergence deactivates wound-induced plant defence against herbivores

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of EIN2 partially restores submergence-suppressed wound-induced expression of JA-biosynthesis/response genes, indicating EIN2-mediated ethylene signaling suppresses JA-dependent defence after submergence.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (ein2-1); RT-qPCR of wound-responsive genes after submergence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN2 is required for part of the submergence-mediated suppression of wound responses. 2. In ein2-1, wound-induced AOC2, JAZ7, and VSP1 expression was partially restored after submergence. 3. EIN2 therefore links ethylene signaling to reduced JA-associated defence under submergence.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated functionally under submergence via ethylene signaling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppressing JA defence may increase herbivore susceptibility after flooding.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA mediates wound and herbivore defence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it is a signaling component rather than a TF or metabolic enzyme. Accession/locus for EIN2 was not provided in the paper text/data-availability list.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Submergence strongly suppresses wound-induced OPR3 expression and is associated with reduced JA accumulation and reduced herbivore defence.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; MeJA treatment response; ChIP-qPCR for histone marks at OPR3 locus; JA quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 is a representative JA biosynthesis gene whose wound induction is strongly repressed by submergence. 2. Short-term submergence reduces active histone acetylation at the OPR3 locus. 3. OPR3 repression correlates with reduced wound-induced JA accumulation and weaker defence against Pieris rapae.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: wound-induced in seedlings and adult plants; suppressed after submergence
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced OPR3 expression lowers JA defence and increases herbivore performance.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Submergence reduces active histone H3 acetylation at the OPR3 locus; H3K4me2 decrease was observed but interpreted cautiously due to control-locus effects. TSA partially restored late wound-induced expression after submergence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OPR3 encodes an enzyme in JA biosynthesis, the paper’s core focus was its regulation under submergence, including epigenetic control; retained here as a core studied gene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid (OPDA) toward jasmonic acid biosynthesis in the octadecanoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: OPC-8:0-related reduced intermediate toward jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of a direct precursor en route to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppressed OPR3 expression after submergence correlates with reduced wound-induced JA accumulation and reduced anti-herbivore defence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; JA metabolite measurement; ChIP-qPCR; MeJA treatment; herbivore assay at pathway-output level
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced by wounding in air; largely suppressed after submergence; MeJA-inducible but still suppressed after submergence
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; treated as an important JA biosynthesis gene responsive to wounding and submergence.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 wound induction is rapidly inhibited by submergence. 2. This repression accompanies decreased JA accumulation. 3. Submergence reduces active histone acetylation at the OPR3 locus, linking epigenetic change to lower JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower JA biosynthesis weakens defence against herbivores after flooding-like conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA promotes wound signalling and herbivore defence.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Submergence lowers H3Ac at the OPR3 locus; TSA partially relieves late repression after submergence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A focal mechanistic gene in the paper due to detailed promoter/chromatin analysis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: allene oxide cyclization step of octadecanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts unstable allene oxide into 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid (OPDA) in JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor of jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wound-induced AOC2 expression is significantly suppressed by submergence; this suppression is partially restored in ein2-1.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important JA biosynthesis gene; rate limitation not established.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC2 is among the JA biosynthesis genes repressed by submergence. 2. EIN2 contributes partially to this repression. 3. Reduced AOC2 expression supports lower JA output after submergence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression may compromise herbivore resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA contributes to wound and anti-herbivore defence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in Results as one of the genes significantly restored in ein2-1 after submergence.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: stress-response transcription associated with ethylene signaling affecting JA-associated wound defence context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABR1 expression is induced by submergence, serving as evidence that rapid ethylene-responsive transcription is activated before strong hypoxia markers appear.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABR1 is rapidly induced by submergence. 2. Its induction supports activation of ethylene signaling under short-term submergence. 3. This provides mechanistic context for ethylene-mediated suppression of wound/JA responses.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Marker of rapid ethylene activation under submergence rather than a directly proven decisive regulator of JA output in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is experimentally measured in Results as part of the submergence-triggered ethylene response, but direct causal control of JA metabolite was not functionally validated.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: stress-response transcription associated with ethylene signaling affecting JA-associated wound defence context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERF4 expression is induced by submergence, supporting activation of ethylene-responsive transcription during early submergence.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF4 is upregulated after submergence. 2. This induction indicates early ethylene response before hypoxia marker induction during short-term submergence. 3. It supports the conclusion that ethylene contributes to suppression of wound-induced JA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Indicator of ethylene pathway activation; decisive causal role on JA reduction not directly tested.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as an ethylene-responsive gene, not directly mutant-validated as a core causal regulator.
